steps.title: Examine Your Conscience
steps.body: Recall your sins since your last confession. Use the examination below, going through each commandment quietly and honestly.

steps.title: Make an Act of Contrition
steps.body: Be truly sorry for having offended God; not merely from fear of punishment, but from love of Him who is all good and deserving of all your love.

steps.title: Resolve to Amend Your Life
steps.body: Firmly resolve to avoid sin and the near occasions of sin. Without this firm purpose of amendment, absolution cannot be validly received.

steps.title: Approach the Confessional
steps.body: Kneel and make the Sign of the Cross. Begin with the words:

formula.latin: ℣. Bless me, Father, for I have sinned. It has been [time] since my last confession. These are my sins:
formula.english: ℟. Confess your sins simply, clearly, and completely. State the kind and number of each mortal sin. You may also confess venial sins.

steps.title: Listen to the Priest's Counsel
steps.body: The priest may offer guidance and assign a penance. Listen attentively; he speaks in the person of Christ.

formula.latin: ℣. O my God, I am heartily sorry for having offended Thee, and I detest all my sins because I dread the loss of Heaven and the pains of Hell; but most of all because they offend Thee, my God, Who art all good and deserving of all my love. I firmly resolve, with the help of Thy grace, to confess my sins, to do penance, and to amend my life. Amen.

steps.title: Receive Absolution
steps.body: The priest extends his hand and pronounces the words of absolution:

formula.latin: ℟. Ego te absólvo a peccátis tuis, in nómine Patris, et Fílii, ✠ et Spíritus Sancti. Amen.
formula.english: ℟. I absolve thee from thy sins, in the name of the Father, and of the Son, ✠ and of the Holy Ghost. Amen.

steps.title: Perform Your Penance
steps.body: Complete the penance assigned by the priest as soon as possible; it is part of the sacrament.

steps.title: Give Thanks to God
steps.body: Spend a moment in thanksgiving. Your soul has been washed clean in the Precious Blood of Christ.

steps.title: Avoid the Occasions of Sin
steps.body: Guard the grace you have received. Flee from persons, places, and things that lead you into temptation.

steps.body: Recall your sins since your last confession. Use the examination below, going through each commandment quietly and honestly.

steps.title: Listen to the Priest's Counsel

steps.title: Pray the Act of Contrition
steps.body: ℣. O my God, I am heartily sorry for having offended Thee, and I detest all my sins because I dread the loss of Heaven and the pains of Hell; but most of all because they offend Thee, my God, Who art all good and deserving of all my love. steps.title: Receive Absolution
steps.body: The priest extends his hand and pronounces the words of absolution: Ego te absólvo a peccátis tuis, in nómine Patris, et Fílii, ✠ et Spíritus Sancti. Amen.; I absolve thee from thy sins, in the name of the Father, and of the Son, ✠ and of the Holy Ghost. Amen.

steps.title: Perform Your Penance
steps.body: Complete the penance assigned by the priest as soon as possible; it is part of the sacrament.

steps.title: Give Thanks to God
steps.body: Spend a moment in thanksgiving. Your soul has been washed clean in the Precious Blood of Christ.

steps.title: Avoid the Occasions of Sin
steps.body: Guard the grace you have received. Flee from persons, places, and things that lead you into temptation.

steps.title: Let your love arise from meditating on the sufferings of Jesus.
See that He suffered because of us, and have true contrition because of Jesus, not because of ourselves.
Cultivate true humility, acknowledging: "I deserve suffering and punishment."
Behold His Most Precious Blood, and let it kindle a supernatural love, a longing to share in His suffering.
Be sorry not only for your own sins, but also for the sins of all people against God.
Let your love arise from meditating on the sufferings of Jesus.
See that He suffered because of us, and have true contrition because of Jesus, not because of ourselves.
Cultivate true humility, acknowledging: "I deserve suffering and punishment."
Behold His Most Precious Blood, and let it kindle a supernatural love, a longing to share in His suffering.
Be sorry not only for your own sins, but also for the sins of all people against God.
Prayer of St. Catherine of Siena
steps.body: O eternal God, O eternal Trinity, through the union of Thy divine nature Thou hast made so precious the Blood of Thine only-begotten Son! O eternal Trinity, Thou art a deep sea in which the more I seek, the more I find; and the more I find, the more I seek Thee. Thou art the fire that ever burns without being consumed. Thou art the fire that consumes in Thy heat every form of self-love in the soul. O eternal Trinity, O Godhead! Thou, O abyss of charity, art mad with love for Thy creature. O eternal Truth, O eternal Fire, O eternal Wisdom! Grant that my soul may ever praise Thee. Clothe me, clothe me with Thyself, O eternal Truth, that I may run the course of this mortal life in true obedience and in the light of most holy faith. Amen.

prayer.label: Oratio S. Catharinæ · A Prayer of St. Catherine
prayer.text:
Holy Spirit, come into my heart,
by your power I journey to You, God,
and grant me charity with fear.

Protect me, O Christ, from every evil thought;
warm me with your sweet love,
so that each burden seems light to me.

My holy Father and my sweet Lord,
help me always in all my endeavours.
Christ love, Christ love.
prayer.attribution: Composed by St. Catherine of Siena at Rocca d'Orcia, 1377, after miraculously having learned to write.
